audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:Welke + noun + ...?]] and [[grammar:noun + van mij/jou/hem/haar]]. Use [[grammar:welke]] before a [[grammar:de-word]] when you ask which one. Put [[grammar:van mij]], [[grammar:van jou]], [[grammar:van hem]], or [[grammar:van haar]] after a noun to identify the owner.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:Welke + noun + ...?]]: [[grammar:Welke]] functions as an interrogative determiner and agrees here with a common-gender count noun.; [[grammar:noun + van mij/jou/hem/haar]]: The possessor is expressed analytically in a postnominal prepositional phrase headed by [[grammar:van]].

audienceSections.normal:
#### Welke + noun + ...?

Use [[grammar:welke]] before a [[grammar:de-word]] when you ask which one. Example: [[grammar:Welke weide kiezen we in het park?]]

#### noun + van mij/jou/hem/haar

Put [[grammar:van mij]], [[grammar:van jou]], [[grammar:van hem]], or [[grammar:van haar]] after a noun to identify the owner. Example: [[grammar:De deken op de stoel is van mij.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### Welke + noun + ...?

[[grammar:Welke]] functions as an interrogative determiner and agrees here with a common-gender count noun. Literal example: [[grammar:Welke weide kiezen we in het park?]]

#### noun + van mij/jou/hem/haar

The possessor is expressed analytically in a postnominal prepositional phrase headed by [[grammar:van]]. Literal example: [[grammar:De deken op de stoel is van mij.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Welke]] is used here with de-words. A [[grammar:van-phrase]] can identify an owner without changing the noun.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Welke]] is used here with de-words. A [[grammar:van-phrase]] can identify an owner without changing the noun.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Morgen is het zonnig. Gaan we picknicken?]] — It is sunny tomorrow. Shall we have a picnic?
- [[grammar:Ja. Welke mand nemen we?]] — Yes. Which basket shall we take?
- [[grammar:De mand bij de deur is van jou, Sophie.]] — The basket by the door is yours, Sophie.
- [[grammar:Klopt. Welke deken nemen we?]] — That’s right. Which blanket shall we take?
- [[grammar:De deken op de stoel is van mij.]] — The blanket on the chair is mine.
- [[grammar:Is de fles van jou, Daan?]] — Is the bottle yours, Daan?
- [[grammar:Ja, de fles is van mij.]] — Yes, the bottle is mine.
- [[grammar:We nemen brood, fruit en water mee.]] — We will take bread, fruit, and water along.
- [[grammar:Welke weide kiezen we in het park?]] — Which meadow shall we choose in the park?
- [[grammar:De weide naast de tuin.]] — The meadow next to the garden.
- [[grammar:Dan draag ik de mand.]] — Then I will carry the basket.
- [[grammar:En ik neem de deken mee.]] — And I will take the blanket along.
